phases.title: Foundations & How Computers Work
phases.description: Understand what a computer actually does before writing any code.

modules.title: Binary, Hex, and Logic Gates
modules.description: How computers represent data and perform basic operations.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Build a Subnet Mask Calculator in C. It must accept an IP address and a CIDR notation, convert them to binary using bitwise operators, and calculate the Network and Broadcast addresses.

modules.title: Memory, Storage, and OS Basics
modules.description: Processes, threads, and how the OS manages hardware.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Write a program that intentionally triggers a 'Stack Overflow' and another that causes a 'Memory Leak' (Heap exhaustion). Explain exactly what happens at the OS level when these crash.

modules.title: The Terminal & Bash Scripting
modules.description: Navigation, Bash scripting, and the power of piping.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Build a 'Log Analyzer' Bash script. It must ingest a 10,000-line Apache access log, use grep/awk/sed to find the top 5 IP addresses that caused 404 errors, and output a clean summary.

modules.title: Git From Scratch
modules.description: Init, commit, branch, and merge fundamentals.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Simulate a team disaster. Create a repo, branch off, intentionally create a severe merge conflict in a vital file, resolve it using standard Git tools, and write a post-mortem commit message.

modules.title: How Compilers Work
modules.description: Tokenizing, parsing, and Abstract Syntax Trees (AST).

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Write a simple Lexer/Tokenizer in C or Python that takes a mathematical equation (e.g., '5 + (10 * 2)') and breaks it down into an array of strictly typed Tokens.

modules.title: Build Your Own Shell (Part 1)
modules.description: Parsing input and handling basic built-in commands.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Start your custom C Shell ('csh'). It must display a custom prompt, read infinite user input without crashing on memory limits, and successfully handle the 'exit' and 'help' built-in commands.
Real-World Project: Upgrade 'csh'. Implement process forking using 'fork()' and 'execvp()'. It must be able to run standard Linux commands like 'ls -la' and 'pwd'.

modules.title: Phase 0 Final Project
modules.description: A full working CLI shell from scratch.

assignment.prompt: Phase 0 Final Project: Complete 'csh'. It must now support input/output redirection (>, <) and piping (|) so users can run commands like 'ls -la | grep txt > output.txt'.

phases.title: Core Programming Languages
phases.description: Be fluent in C++, Python, and JavaScript at a deep level.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Build a high-performance Image Matrix manipulator using raw pointers to invert colors without duplicating the array in memory.
Real-World Project: Build an RPG inventory system. Use inheritance and polymorphism to handle Weapons, Potions, and Armor, calculating total weight and stats.
Real-World Project: Build a custom 'Smart Pointer' class template from scratch that mimics std::unique_ptr to prevent memory leaks.
Real-World Project: Build a robust CSV parser that reads a 100MB dataset, handles malformed rows with custom Exception classes, and outputs a clean JSON file.
Real-World Project: Build a Financial Data Cleaner. Use list/dict comprehensions and lambda functions to sanitize messy bank transaction strings into clean numeric floats.
Real-World Project: Build a modular CLI for managing AWS servers (mocked). Use class-based architecture to separate authentication, server actions, and logging.
Real-World Project: Build a Configuration Manager that loads from a JSON file, validates schema constraints, and gracefully falls back to default settings if the file is corrupted.
Real-World Project: Build a Slack Bot. Use the 'requests' library to fetch data from the GitHub API and automatically post a daily summary of open Pull Requests to a Slack channel.
Real-World Project: Implement a custom 'debounce' and 'throttle' utility function using closures to optimize a simulated search bar input.
Real-World Project: Build a 'Promise.all' polyfill from scratch to deeply understand concurrent execution, then use it to fetch user data from 3 separate mock APIs simultaneously.
Real-World Project: Build a Kanban Board (like Trello) in pure Vanilla JS. Implement drag-and-drop functionality using native DOM events.
Phase 1 Final Project: Build an offline-first Note Taking app. It must fetch initial data from an API, save changes to LocalStorage, and sync back when the network reconnects.

phases.title: Fullstack Web Development
phases.description: Build production-grade web apps from first principles.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Code a fully semantic, WCAG-compliant landing page for a law firm. Score 100 on Google Lighthouse for Accessibility and SEO.
Real-World Project: Recreate the complex grid layout of the Netflix homepage using only CSS Grid and Flexbox (no frameworks).
Real-World Project: Build a SaaS Dashboard that is perfectly usable on a 320px phone screen and expands into a multi-column layout on 4K monitors.
Real-World Project: Build a highly interactive loading sequence (spinner, progress bar, success checkmark) using keyframes and hardware-accelerated CSS properties (transform/opacity).
Real-World Project: Write a raw TCP client in Python that manually constructs an HTTP GET request, sends it to a server, and parses the raw HTTP response headers.
Real-World Project: Design a comprehensive OpenAPI/Swagger specification for a Ride-Sharing app (Uber clone), detailing endpoints for Users, Drivers, and Trips.
Real-World Project: Build a fully functional HTTP server using ONLY the native Node.js 'http' module (no Express) that serves HTML files and a JSON API.
Real-World Project: Build your own mini-Express routing framework. It must support middleware chains (e.g., authentication -> logging -> controller) and route parameters.
Real-World Project: Write complex SQL queries for an E-commerce database to find 'Customers who bought item X but never bought item Y', utilizing JOINs and Subqueries.
Real-World Project: Take a massive, unnormalized spreadsheet of Hospital Records and design a 3rd Normal Form (3NF) relational schema.
Real-World Project: Build a backend API for a Library system using Node.js and the 'pg' package. Implement robust connection pooling and SQL injection prevention (parameterized queries).
Real-World Project: Design the database architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS application (like Slack). Choose between shared database vs. isolated schemas and justify it.
Real-World Project: Build a production-ready Express API for a blogging platform. Include error handling middleware, request validation using Zod, and rate limiting.
Real-World Project: Build a Cryptocurrency Tracker in React. Use functional components and state to display live prices fetching from an external API.
Real-World Project: Build a complex multi-step Checkout Form. Create custom hooks like 'useLocalStorage' to persist form data and 'useForm' to handle validation logic.
Real-World Project: Connect your React Frontend to your Express/Postgres backend to build a complete Real Estate listing platform with filtering and search.
Real-World Project: Implement secure JWT authentication from scratch. Generate tokens on login, store them securely in HttpOnly cookies, and protect specific API routes.
Real-World Project: Add 'Login with Google' and 'Login with GitHub' to your application using the standard OAuth 2.0 flow (without using a heavy library like Passport initially).
Real-World Project: Conduct a security audit on a intentionally vulnerable web app. Identify and patch XSS, CSRF, and SQL Injection vulnerabilities.
Real-World Project: Integrate Stripe Checkout for payments and AWS S3 (via presigned URLs) for profile picture uploads.
Real-World Project: Take a bloated React application and optimize it using Code Splitting (React.lazy), Memoization (useMemo), and image optimization to achieve a 90+ Lighthouse score.
Real-World Project: Build an interactive 3D landing page hero section. Render a 3D Earth that rotates based on the user's scroll position.
Real-World Project: Build a 3D Car Configurator. Allow users to change the paint color (metallic vs matte) and rims using advanced PBR materials and environment maps.
Phase 2 Final Project: Build a fully functional 3D Web Portfolio. Navigate through a 3D museum of your projects using Raycasting for interaction and Tween.js for camera movement.

phases.title: IoT & Embedded Systems
phases.description: From zero electronics knowledge to cloud-connected IoT systems.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project (Wokwi): Design a fail-safe relay circuit where two separate buttons must be pressed simultaneously to activate a high-power LED, demonstrating AND logic in hardware.

Real-World Project (Wokwi): Write a robust sensor driver that reads the DHT22. It must include error handling for NaN values and implement a 'Moving Average' filter to smooth out sudden temperature spikes.

Real-World Project (Wokwi): Build a 'Smart Blinds' prototype. Map a Light Dependent Resistor (simulated via pot) to a servo. As the light gets brighter, the servo automatically closes the blinds smoothly.
Real-World Project: Draw a professional schematic for an automated plant watering system (ESP32, Soil Sensor, Water Pump) adhering to industry standards (VCC up, GND down, proper net labels).
Real-World Project (Wokwi): Write a 'Breathing LED' script that perfectly mimics the sleep indicator of an old Macbook using sine wave math and PWM, without using the delay() function.

Real-World Project (Wokwi): Build a highly resilient Wi-Fi connection manager. It must attempt to connect, and if it fails after 10 seconds, it should gracefully fall back to creating its own Access Point (AP mode).
Real-World Project: Build a precise RPM counter for a motor. Use an External Hardware Interrupt to count rotations and a Hardware Timer to calculate and print the RPM exactly every 1.000 seconds.
Real-World Project: Build an Industrial Monitoring Panel. Read 3 sensors asynchronously, prioritize critical alarms (Interrupts), and display the data on an I2C OLED without the screen flickering.
Real-World Project: Program two microcontrollers to act as a secure vault system. MCU 1 reads a keypad and sends an encrypted UART payload to MCU 2, which validates it and opens a lock.
Real-World Project: Build a weather station that reads from an I2C BME280 (Temp/Pressure) and an I2C BH1750 (Light) simultaneously, handling potential bus collision errors gracefully.
Real-World Project: Build a High-Speed Flight Data Recorder. Log accelerometer data to an SD card via SPI at 100Hz, ensuring file buffer flushes prevent data loss on sudden power failure.

Real-World Project: Build a 'Smart Fleet' tracker. The ESP32 must publish its status to an MQTT broker. Implement a 'Last Will and Testament' so the dashboard instantly knows if the tracker goes offline.
Real-World Project: Build an ESP32 Crypto Ticker. It must make an HTTPS GET request to the CoinGecko API, verify the SSL certificate fingerprint, parse the JSON, and display the Bitcoin price.
Real-World Project: Build a 'Smart Switch'. The ESP32 must subscribe to a Firebase RTDB stream. When you toggle a button on your phone, the ESP32 must react instantly (under 200ms).

Real-World Project: Build a Next.js command center for your IoT devices. It must display live graphs of Firebase data using Recharts and allow the user to send configuration updates back to the ESP32.
Real-World Project: Build an Early-Warning Flood Detection system. If the water sensor triggers, a Next.js API route must immediately dispatch a high-priority alert to your phone via a Telegram Bot.
Real-World Project: Implement an OTA Update Server. The ESP32 checks a version endpoint on boot. If a newer firmware exists, it downloads it securely over HTTPS, verifies it, and reboots.
Real-World Project: Build a Solar-Powered Remote Weather Station. Utilize ESP32 Deep Sleep to drop power draw to 10uA, waking up only once an hour to transmit data, calculating a 5-year battery life.

Real-World Project: Architect a Smart City Waste Management system (10,000 bins). Deliver a whitepaper detailing your choice of network (LoRaWAN vs Cellular), database scaling strategy, and edge intelligence.
Phase 3 Final Project: Build a commercial-grade IoT product. It must include: Hardware Watchdogs, Offline Data Syncing, Over-the-Air updates, and a fully polished Next.js user dashboard.

phases.title: Game Development
phases.description: Build games from scratch — understand game loops, physics, 3D.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Build a frame-rate independent physics engine core in JS. Prove that a ball drops at the exact same speed whether the loop is artificially throttled to 15 FPS or runs at 144 FPS.
Real-World Project: Build a 'Bullet Hell' avoidance game. Implement Spatial Partitioning (Grid) so the game can smoothly calculate collisions for 5,000 simultaneous bullets without dropping frames.
Real-World Project: Build a complex Player Controller using a Finite State Machine. Implement 'Input Buffering' and 'Coyote Time' to make platforming jumps feel incredibly responsive and fair.
Real-World Project: Build an 'Angry Birds' style slingshot mechanic. Use Verlet Integration to simulate a realistic, stable rope/elastic band that pulls a physics body.
Real-World Project: Build an interactive 3D Solar System. Implement point lights for the sun, and accurately rotate planets and moons on their own distinct Z-axes using hierarchical scene graphs.

Real-World Project: Build a 3D First-Person 'Escape Room'. Use raycasting against specific collision groups (not the whole scene) to prevent the player from walking through walls or falling out of bounds.

Real-World Project: Implement a Third-Person Character Controller. Load a Mixamo GLTF model and seamlessly cross-fade animations from 'Idle' to 'Walk' to 'Run' based on the analog magnitude of user input.

Phase 4 Final Project: Release a polished 3D Web Game. It must feature spatial audio (Web Audio API), post-processing bloom effects, screen shake on impact, and optimized assets (Draco compression) loading under 3 seconds.

phases.title: Business & Freelancing
phases.description: Turn your skills into a sustainable income.

assignment.prompt: Real-World Project: Calculate your Minimum Viable Rate based on your specific life expenses. Then, write a 'Value-Based Pricing' pitch to convince a skeptical client why your $10,000 fee is a good investment.

Real-World Project: Draft a bulletproof Freelance Master Services Agreement (MSA). Ensure it explicitly defines IP ownership transfer ONLY upon final payment, and sets strict communication boundaries.
Real-World Project: Identify an underserved niche (e.g., local dental clinics). Build a targeted Case Study portfolio piece, and execute a cold outreach campaign via LinkedIn to 5 decision-makers.

Phase 5 Final Project: Set up a comprehensive Business Operating System. Establish separate banking, a tax-withholding automation rule (30%), and build a 12-month financial runway projection spreadsheet.
